name: Roquefort Cheesecake (Bon Appetit/July 1983)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Butter a 9-inch springform pan.
3. Mix breadcrumbs and Parmesan. Sprinkle the mixture in the pan, turning to coat evenly. Refrigerate.
4. Fry bacon in a heavy medium skillet until crisp. Remove with tongs and drain on paper towels.
5. Pour off all but about 1 tablespoon of bacon fat from the skillet.
6. Add onion and cover; cook over low heat until translucent, stirring occasionally for about 10 minutes. Crumble the bacon.
7. In a blender or food processor, mix cream cheese and Roquefort until smooth.
8. Add eggs, whipping cream, salt, and hot pepper sauce; process until smooth.
9. Blend in the crumbled bacon and onion; the filling should retain some texture.
10. Pour into the prepared pan.
11. Set the pan in a roasting pan. Add enough hot water to come halfway up the sides of the springform pan.
12. Bake at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for 1 hour and 20 minutes.
13. Turn off the oven; let the cheesecake cool with the door slightly open for about an hour.
14. Transfer to a rack; allow it to cool to room temperature before removing from the pan and serving.
